concept: Integrate / Bring Into Wholeness / Unify Parts
description: To bring separate elements into a functioning whole, creating coherent unity from what was fragmented. 'Shiwolu' names the work of integration—whether healing parts of self, incorporating new members into community, or synthesizing ideas into understanding.
sound_symbolism: The word weaves together. 'Shi' /ʃi/ begins with hushing focus, attending to what needs joining. Then 'wo' opens with wondering care, seeing how parts fit. Finally 'lu' flows with liquid continuity, integration complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mi lo themo shiwolu' (I integrate something). Can be reflexive: 'mi shiwolu' (I integrate, become whole). Related to koluma (unite) but emphasizes functional coherence rather than simple joining.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Integrated systems mimic nature's efficiency—when parts work together harmoniously, the whole thrives.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Integration of experience—making peace with all parts of ourselves—is essential for wholeness and authentic presence.
